description: Implement P2 (Task Complexity Routing) and P3 (Incremental Context) - new complexity_assessor module and incremental context assembly
steps: P2 - Create kernel/complexity_assessor.py with function assess_complexity(goal: str) -> str returning 'low', 'medium', or 'high'. Logic: low if goal contains keywords (hello world, single file, config, fix, repair, simple) in Chinese/English or len < 20 chars; high if contains (architecture, system, multi-module, microservice, distributed, refactor entire) in Chinese/English or len > 100; medium otherwise.
P2 - Add --complexity CLI argument to runner.py parse_args(): choices=['auto','low','medium','high'], default='auto'.
P2 - In runner.py main(), after goal is set and before the main loop: if args.complexity == 'auto', call assess_complexity(goal). Store complexity level in state.
P2 - For low complexity: set state current_node to 'code' directly (skip init and plan). Generate a minimal task in memory/tasks.yaml with the goal as a single task. The code node will pick it up.

P2 - For medium complexity: after review_pass transition, instead of going to reflect, go directly to plan (skip reflect and evolve). Implement by intercepting the transition in the Mode 3 loop: if complexity=='medium' and transition_condition=='review_pass', override next_node to 'plan' instead of 'reflect'.
P2 - For high complexity: no changes, full flow as-is.
P3 - Add instance variables to ContextAssembler: self._last_node_id = None, self._iteration_count = 0, self._last_full_context_hash = None.

P3 - Add method assemble_incremental(state, node, graph_executor, knowledge_store) -> str. Logic: if self._last_node_id == node['id'] and self._iteration_count > 0, build a reduced prompt with: output_format, node prompt, state summary (always), plus a delta section showing what changed since last iteration (new task, files changed, etc).

P3 - In runner.py Mode 3, track prev_node_id. If prev_node_id == current node_id and previous iteration was successful, call assembler.assemble_incremental() instead of assembler.assemble(). If incremental assembly fails or returns empty, fall back to full.
P3 - The incremental prompt should include: '=== INCREMENTAL UPDATE ===' header, the output_format section, the node prompt, the state summary, and any changed task info.
Add tests in tests/test_complexity_assessor.py: test low/medium/high keyword detection, test character length thresholds, test Chinese keywords.
Add tests in tests/test_incremental_context.py: test that assemble_incremental returns reduced content on same-node repeat, test first call returns full context, test node change resets to full.
Add tests for --complexity CLI arg parsing and routing logic in tests/test_task_routing.py.
Run full test suite: python3.12 -m pytest tests/ --cov --cov-fail-under=90 -q
acceptance_criteria: assess_complexity('Build a distributed microservice architecture') returns 'high'
--complexity auto correctly calls assess_complexity and routes
Low complexity skips init/plan, starts at code node
Medium complexity skips reflect/evolve after review_pass
assemble_incremental() returns smaller output than assemble() on consecutive same-node calls
findings: All 1157 tests pass (1 pre-existing failure in test_knowledge.py unrelated to this work). Coverage at 98.36%. Updated 8 existing test files to add --complexity high to prevent the auto-detection from interfering with short test goals (< 20 chars). The incremental context is most beneficial for nodes that already have tiered content (like init), since code-node full context is already small due to tiering.
